events.title: Prayer Night
events.subtitle: Glacier Point 160
events.description: Join us for Chi Alpha Prayer at UC Merced! Fridays at 7 pm in GLACIER 160, we gather to seek God together through prayer, worship, and encouragement. It's a peaceful space to recharge, connect with God, and support one another. All are welcome, whether you're new to prayer or have been walking with Jesus for years.

events.title: Chi Alpha Encounters Spring
events.subtitle: PAV Lawn
events.description: This event provides a unique opportunity for students to deepen their personal relationship with Jesus while also building a strong community with their peers. Whether you are new to faith or have been on this journey for a while, there is also an opportunity for you to learn more about who Jesus is in a supportive environment.

events.title: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting
events.subtitle: BSP 101
events.description: Agricultural Insects and Plants Monthly Meeting is a regular meeting where graduate and undergraduate students discuss insects and plants that are important in agriculture. Participants talk about insects that damage crops, beneficial insects such as pollinators and natural enemies, and how plants respond to insect feeding. They share research findings, field observations, and practical, environmentally responsible pest management approaches. The group also discusses potential future events, outreach activities, career paths, and related academic or professional opportunities. The overall purpose is to exchange knowledge and improve how agricultural systems are understood and managed.

events.title: ASBMB Game Night & Scrapbooking!
events.subtitle: SSB 160
events.description: Come unwind and connect with fellow members at our ASBMB Game Night & Scrapbooking event! Test your wits in a fun Jeopardy-style game, get creative with our scrapbooking project, and enjoy great conversation in a relaxed, friendly atmosphere.

events.title: Un Sueño en Mexico
events.subtitle: Merced Theater 301 W Main Street Merced CA, 935340
events.description: Ballet Folklorico de UCM is hosting their anual end of the year showcase. This event showcases the beauty of Mexican culture through the tradition of folk dances that hold significance and meaning to our culture. This is a three-hour long event of dancing and music where our club members will be on stage dancing to over 20 songs that they have learned throughout the fall and spring semester. This showcase not only demonstrates the cultural traditions of Mexican folk dance, but it also shows the dedication and the hard work of our club members. It's an opportunity for our members to grow their performance skills by allowing them to dance on a stage in a theater that will allow them to show all they have learned in regards to keeping the Mexican tradition alive through dance.

events.title: Sigma Chi Province Workshop
events.subtitle: COB 105, COB 110, COB 114, COB 113, COB 263, COB 267, COB 265
events.description: Sigma Chi – California Central Valley Province Workshop. We’re looking forward to bringing brothers together for a focused and productive day of leadership development, collaboration, and best-practice sharing across the province. Brothers are invited from across the Central Valley province, and will be visiting for the day.The event will begin with all the invited chapters, coming together to go through a small workshop, and then going into breakout room intermittently. One large room is going to be reserved, as well as 7 breakout rooms.

events.title: Voices & Values: AI for Language Justice Mini-Conference
events.subtitle: Fred & Mitzie Ruiz Administration Building, UC Merced
events.description: Voices and Values: AI for Language Justice Mini ConferenceWednesday, April 22 | 9:00 AM–1:30 PM | Admin 306UC Merced students are invited to a half‑day conference exploring how AI can advance language justice and support more inclusive, multilingual communities. This event offers a valuable opportunity to build leadership, communication, and career‑ready skills while connecting with campus and community partners.What to Expect:• Opening remarks and breakfast in Admin 306• Workshops and conversations on AI, accessibility, and equity• Networking with faculty, staff, alumni, and community partners• Breakfast and lunch provided• Parking available for off‑campus partnersRegister here: Voices & Values: AI for Language Justice | Language Accessibility Committee

events.title: UC Merced SIAM Student Chapter: AMA with Industry Experts
events.subtitle: COB1 - 116
events.description: The Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics will be hosting the following industry experts via Zoom virtually: Nessy Tania, Principal Quantitative Systems Pharmacologist at Pfizer and Dr. Marta D’Elia, Director of AI and ModSim at Atomic Machines and adjunct professor at Stanford University’s Institute for Computational and Mathematical Engineering. The event will be a Q&A session, and we invite questions about how to navigate the job market and to learn more about opportunities in the AI space.
